intro: Hi! I'm the AI Upscaler support assistant. I answer from every support topic we've ever handled. Ask me about install errors, GPU not being used, Docker/NAS setup, API tokens, models, and more.

entries.title: Plugin won't install / checksum mismatch
entries.answer:
Jellyfin verifies the plugin ZIP's MD5 against the repository manifest and rejects it on any mismatch.
1. Make sure you use the official repo URL: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Kuschel-code/JellyfinUpscalerPlugin/main/repository-jellyfin.json`
2. Every release since v1.6.1.12 is Quad-MD5 verified, so a checksum error on a current version almost always means a **stale cached repo**. Remove the repository, re-add it, restart Jellyfin, then install the latest (**v1.7.8**).
If it still fails, paste the exact error and your Jellyfin version.

entries.title: "No newer version available" / can't update
entries.answer:
Jellyfin caches the plugin catalog. To force a refresh:
Dashboard → Plugins → **Repositories** → remove the AI Upscaler repo → re-add it → **restart Jellyfin**. The Catalog then shows the latest (**v1.7.8**). A hard browser refresh of the dashboard also helps.

entries.title: Plugin not supported / not compatible with my Jellyfin
entries.answer:
The plugin targets Jellyfin **ABI 10.11.8**. Install the plugin version that matches your Jellyfin:
- Jellyfin 10.11.x → install **v1.7.8**.
- Older Jellyfin → update Jellyfin first.
If the plugin shows as disabled after install, check the Jellyfin logs for the ABI line and update accordingly.

entries.title: Unable to connect to the Docker AI service / service unavailable
entries.answer:
The plugin talks to the Docker AI container over HTTP. Check, in order:
1. The container is **running**.
2. Port **5000** is published (`-p 5000:5000` / `ports: - "5000:5000"`).
3. Open `http://YOUR-SERVER-IP:5000/health` in a browser → it should return `{"status":"healthy", ...}`.
4. In the plugin set **AI Service URL** = `http://YOUR-SERVER-IP:5000`.
A firewall or reverse proxy between Jellyfin and the container can block it.

entries.title: GPU is detected but inference runs on CPU (slow)
entries.answer:
Usually you're on an **old/wrong image**. If `/gpu-verify` shows `AzureExecutionProvider`, that's plain `onnxruntime` with no GPU provider.
1. Force a fresh pull of the right vendor image:
```
docker compose pull
docker compose up -d --force-recreate
```
with `docker7-intel` / `docker7-amd` / `docker7` (NVIDIA).
2. Pass the GPU device: `--gpus all` (NVIDIA), `/dev/dri` (Intel/AMD Linux), or `/dev/dxg` (WSL2). On native Linux also add `group_add: render`.
v1.7.8 also fixed a reporting bug where `/gpu-verify` showed `using_gpu:false` even though OpenVINO was active — trust `active_providers`.

entries.title: Intel Arc / iGPU on Windows or WSL2 not used
entries.answer:
Use the **`docker7-intel`** image (it bundles `onnxruntime-openvino`).
- On WSL2 / Docker Desktop pass **`/dev/dxg`** (not `/dev/dri/renderD128`) and mount `/usr/lib/wsl`.
- Force a fresh image: `docker compose pull` (plain `docker compose up` reuses the cached old image).
`/gpu-verify` should then show `OpenVINOExecutionProvider`. Note: real-time 4x on HD is heavy on an A380 — use **batch** mode for best quality.

entries.title: ONNX error / "Reshape" / wrong input type / FP16 crash
entries.answer:
Two separate fixes — update to the **v1.7.8** images:
- **FP16-on-FP32 warmup crash** (INVALID_ARGUMENT): fixed in v1.7.4 — FP16 is only used when the loaded model actually agrees.
- **"Reshape" warmup error** on `realesrgan-x4-256`: fixed in v1.7.8 — the benchmark now reads the model's real input shape instead of forcing a 64px tile.

entries.title: "Api token not configured" message
entries.answer:
The Docker AI service is **secure-by-default** and refuses requests without a token. Two options:
- **Home / LAN (simplest):** add `API_TOKEN=disable` to the container environment, then restart it.
- **Secured:** set the same secret on **both** sides — container `API_TOKEN=your-secret` and the plugin's **AI Service Token** field.
After either, the message is gone and model downloads work.

entries.title: Non-admin users can't use the plugin (models won't load)
entries.answer: Fixed in **v1.7.5**: 47 of 52 endpoints used to be admin-only (`RequiresElevation`), so normal users could see models but not load/use them. 31 endpoints were reclassified to normal authenticated access. **Update the plugin to v1.7.8** and non-admin users can use it.

entries.title: 4:3 video is stretched to fill the screen
entries.answer: Fixed in **v1.7.5** — both real-time renderers now use `object-fit: contain` on the canvas overlay, so 4:3 sources are letterboxed instead of stretched. Update the plugin to v1.7.8.

entries.title: AMD GPU support (ROCm / Vulkan)
entries.answer:
Two paths:
- **`docker7-amd`** (ROCm). v1.7.8 fixed a bug where it silently ran on CPU (plain onnxruntime was shadowing the ROCm build) — it now reports `ROCMExecutionProvider`.
- **`docker7-vulkan`** (ncnn-Vulkan) — works on most AMD/Intel GPUs and is the reliable path for older AMD cards.
Pass `/dev/dri` (+ `/dev/kfd` for ROCm) to the container.

entries.title: NVIDIA / RTX random error or crash
entries.answer:
Use the **`docker7`** (NVIDIA) image with `--gpus all` and the NVIDIA Container Toolkit installed on the host.
If you saw an `INVALID_ARGUMENT` error at warmup, that was the FP16/FP32 bug fixed in v1.7.4. Update to the **v1.7.8** images. `/gpu-verify` should show `CUDAExecutionProvider` / `TensorrtExecutionProvider`.

entries.title: Real-time keeps falling back to WebGL / poor playback performance
entries.answer:
Real-time **server** upscaling is best-effort: if your GPU can't sustain the target FPS within the per-frame budget, the plugin drops back to client-side WebGL so playback doesn't stutter. Live **4x on HD** exceeds most consumer GPUs (even an Arc A380).
For real quality, use the **batch / scheduled-task** route — pre-process the file once, then play the upscaled result normally.

entries.title: Batch upscale job stuck at 95% / where are the output files?
entries.answer:
95% is the final **re-mux** stage (re-encode video + copy audio/subs).
- The finished file is `<name>_upscaled.<ext>` next to the source; a failed/partial output is **auto-deleted** (so you won't find leftovers).
- Intermediate frames live in the container's `/tmp` (not mounted, so invisible from the host).
If it hangs at 95%, `/tmp` likely filled up — mount a volume: `- ai-tmp:/tmp`. Grab `docker logs --tail 80` + `df -h /tmp` while it's stuck.

entries.title: Which model should I use?
entries.answer:
From the 71-model catalog:
- **Fast / low-VRAM:** `realesr-general-x4v3` (~5MB), `lsdir-compact-v2-x4`, `spanx2-ch48`.
- **Quality:** `realesrgan-x4`, `nomos2-dat2-x4`, `drct-l-x4`, `nomos8k-hat-l-x4` (heavy).
- **Compressed / streaming sources:** `realwebphoto-v4-dat2-x4`, `nomoswebphoto-realplksr-x4`.
- **Anime:** `real-cugan-x4`, `apisr-anime-x2`.
See the Model catalog page for the full list.

entries.title: Setup on ZimaOS / CasaOS / Synology / TrueNAS / Unraid
entries.answer:
Deploy the AI service with **docker compose** (one file, survives reboots). Minimal:
```
services:
  ai-upscaler:
    image: kuscheltier/jellyfin-ai-upscaler:docker7-cpu
    ports: ["5000:5000"]
    environment: ["API_TOKEN=disable"]
    volumes: ["ai-models:/app/models"]
    restart: unless-stopped
volumes:
  ai-models:
```
Use `docker7-intel` (+ `/dev/dri`) for an Intel iGPU. Then set the plugin's **AI Service URL** to `http://NAS-IP:5000`. TrueNAS users: there's a ready compose in `docker-ai-service/truenas/`.

entries.title: Scan only specific libraries (not everything)
entries.answer: Implemented in **v1.6.1.14**: the Settings tab has a library picker (stored as `EnabledLibraryIds`). Tick the libraries you want the scheduled scan to process. Leave all unticked = scan everything (the default).

entries.title: Old docker5 / docker6 tag missing
entries.answer: The old tag families (`docker4/5/6/6.1`) are retired and were cleaned up. Use the current rolling tags **`docker7-<backend>`** (e.g. `docker7-intel`) or pin a release: **`:v1.7.8-<backend>`**.

entries.title: Getting started / is the plugin usable?
entries.answer:
The quick path:
1. Deploy the **Docker AI service** (compose — see the NAS/setup answer).
2. Install the plugin from the repo and point its **AI Service URL** at `http://server:5000`.
3. Open `http://server:5000` and **download a model**.
Then: **batch** mode upscales your library overnight; **real-time** mode enhances during playback. See the Installation and Configuration pages.
